questions.prompt: Implicit processes are best described as:
Which best distinguishes implicit attitudes from stereotypes?
options: Attitudes are associations; stereotypes are evaluations
Attitudes are evaluative; stereotypes link groups with traits
Attitudes are always conscious; stereotypes are not

questions.prompt: The Implicit Association Test (IAT) primarily measures:
Which limitation of the IAT was discussed in lecture?
options: It cannot measure any bias at all
It only predicts behaviour perfectly at the individual level
It is better at predicting average group behaviour than individual behaviour

questions.prompt: Which example best reflects implicit bias in healthcare?
options: A doctor consciously refuses to treat a patient
A doctor unknowingly spends less time with certain patients
A doctor follows all procedures equally

questions.prompt: Which outcome is associated with implicit gender bias in healthcare?
options: Delayed diagnosis for certain conditions in women

questions.prompt: Which best reflects implicit bias in education?
options: Teachers unknowingly having different expectations for students

questions.prompt: Which statement about implicit bias is most accurate?
options: It only exists in people with explicit prejudice
It can exist even without conscious awareness
It has no effect on behaviour
It is fixed and cannot change

questions.prompt: Which best explains why implicit bias forms?
Bias in AI systems can occur because:
options: AI has no access to human data
AI systems learn from biased data and patterns
